actors.name: Giorgia Meloni
actors.blurb: Meloni continues to dominate Italian political discourse with her government's labor reforms, fiscal policies, and responses to judicial controversies. Her recent labor decree, tying nearly €1 billion in incentives to 'fair wages,' has sparked debates about economic priorities and social equity. Additionally, her stance on EU fiscal rules and the Stability Pact remains a contentious issue within her coalition and with EU officials. Meloni's handling of the Nicole Minetti pardon case has also drawn criticism, further polarizing public opinion.

actors.name: Donald Trump
actors.blurb: Trump remains the defining figure of the global right-wing news cycle, with his administration's policies and personal controversies generating intense media scrutiny. Recent coverage has focused on his administration's foreign policy moves, including the Iran conflict and deportation policies, as well as domestic issues like the proposed White House ballroom and responses to assassination attempts. Trump's interactions with international leaders, such as King Charles III, and his ongoing feuds with media figures like Jimmy Kimmel have also kept him in the spotlight.

actors.name: Santiago Abascal
actors.blurb: Abascal continues to drive Vox's aggressive political strategy, particularly around the 'national priority' principle, which has become a central theme in regional coalition agreements with the PP. His confrontational rhetoric, including recent insults directed at Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez, has intensified political tensions in Spain. Vox's influence in regional governments, such as Aragón and Extremadura, has sparked debates about the party's impact on national cohesion and social policies.

actors.name: Péter Magyar
actors.blurb: Magyar has rapidly emerged as a dominant figure in Hungarian politics following his party's landslide victory in the recent elections. His government's plans to reset relations with Ukraine, audit past government spending, and introduce new economic policies have generated significant attention. Magyar's confrontations with former Fidesz officials, including Viktor Orbán, and his efforts to distance Hungary from Russia have positioned him as a key actor in Central European geopolitics.

actors.name: Karol Nawrocki
actors.blurb: Nawrocki has become a central figure in Polish politics, particularly regarding national security and foreign policy. His recent actions, including the declassification of a controversial annex to the WSI report and his role in securing the release of journalist Andrzej Poczobut from Belarus, have sparked debates about transparency and Poland's international standing. Nawrocki's proposals for constitutional changes and his interactions with far-right groups have also drawn significant attention.

actors.name: Marine Le Pen
actors.blurb: Le Pen remains a dominant force in French politics, with her strategic positioning for the 2027 presidential election generating significant coverage. Her preference for a runoff against centrist Édouard Philippe over far-left rival Jean-Luc Mélenchon reflects her efforts to broaden her appeal. Le Pen's recent criticisms of President Macron's rhetoric and her party's stance on issues like immigration, public media, and economic sovereignty continue to shape France's political landscape.
signals: public media and freedom of speech

actors.name: Jordan Bardella
actors.blurb: Bardella continues to rise in prominence within France's far-right political scene, particularly due to his high-profile relationship with Princess Maria Carolina de Bourbon and his strategic positioning ahead of the 2027 presidential election. His leadership in the National Rally and his influence on the party's policies, especially regarding immigration and EU relations, have made him a key figure in shaping France's political future. Bardella's recent popularity dip following media coverage of his personal life has also drawn attention.

actors.name: Matteo Salvini
actors.role: Deputy Prime Minister of Italy and Leader of Lega
actors.blurb: Salvini remains a prominent figure in Italian politics, particularly through his advocacy for infrastructure projects, law enforcement policies, and controversial stances on social issues. His recent focus on e-scooter regulations, support for the 'forest family' case, and criticism of judicial decisions have kept him in the news. Salvini's role within the Italian government and his interactions with coalition partners continue to influence Italy's political direction.
